title: This Is The First Real Shape Of AGI: Fusion Agents
summary: AI Revolution argues that multi-agent fusion systems represent a fundamental shift beyond reasoning to real-world execution, marking the first architectural shape of AGI. The piece cites Abacus AI's ChatLLM and DeepAgent products as examples of agents that coordinate work across multiple workers and deploy functional outputs like apps, dashboards, and services.
writeup: The video makes the case that fusion agents and multi-agent systems mark the first real shape of AGI by extending AI past reasoning into execution: building tools, coordinating work across multiple agents, and deploying usable services. It treats this shift as more consequential than individual model launches because it shows a complete working layer assembled around reasoning capabilities. Key concepts include multi-agent systems, loop agents, reasoning, and AI execution.
